title: leucheria viscida
intro: Leucheria viscida, also known as viscid broom or sticky broom, is a shrub native to New Zealand, belonging to the family Asteraceae. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Leucheria viscida include the treatment of skin conditions, wounds, and respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs. Herbal preparations of Leucheria viscida include teas, infusions, and topical ointments, which are often used to soothe and calm irritated skin and respiratory tissues.
